Name: Application name and geographic location additional contexts are disabled on MFA
Description:
This indicator checks if the Microsoft Authenticator App settings are configured to display the name of the target application and geographical location of the request when a user receives a push notification.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Push notification bombing is a tactic where an attacker will spam a user with excessive push notifications; users who are enabled for passwordless through the Microsoft Authenticator App can receive these with just knowing their username. While number matching in Entra ID primarily prevents users from accepting malicious push notifications, users can have better insight into the requests with additional context. When additional context is enabled, a user will see the geographical location from where the request came, as well as the name of the requesting application. Users can use this to verify that the request is legitimate.

References

Use additional context in Microsoft Authenticator notifications - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Application name and geographic location additional contexts on MFA are disabled.
Remediation:
Enable Show application name in push and passwordless notifications and Show geographic location in push and passwordless notifications in Entra ID.

The configuration for additional context settings passwords can be found in Authentication methods - Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link).

To browse to the settings in the Microsoft Entra admin center, expand the Protection menu on the left, select Authentication methods, and then Policies. Once in Authentication methods, select Microsoft Authenticator on the right blade. The Microsoft Authenticator settings blade will open, and from the top menu select Configure. In the list of settings the status for Show application name in push and passwordless notifications and Show geographic location in push and passwordless notifications should both be changed to Enabled. The Target for the settings should be Include and set to All users.
